title: Chicken Khichuri
description: A comforting one-pot meal perfect for cold nights and exam seasons. Nutritious and filling.
steps: Sauté sliced onions and garlic until golden.
Add chicken and turmeric, cooking until chicken is seared.
Stir in washed rice and lentils.
Add 3 cups of water and simmer until water is absorbed and rice is soft.

title: Egg Fried Rice
description: The ultimate dorm staple. Quick, cheap, and uses only one pan.
steps: Heat oil in a pan and scramble the eggs.
Add diced carrots and sauté for 2 minutes.
Toss in cooked rice and soy sauce.
Stir-fry on high heat for 3-5 minutes.

title: Veggie Noodles
description: A light and spicy noodle dish packed with seasonal vegetables.
steps: Boil noodles and drain.
Sauté cabbage and chili in oil.
Add seasoning and noodles.
Mix well and serve hot.

title: One-Pot Pasta
description: No straining required. Pasta and sauce cook together for maximum flavor.
steps: Combine pasta, sauce, and water in a pot.
Bring to a boil, then simmer for 10-12 minutes.
Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.
Top with cheese and oregano.

title: Peanut Butter Banana Toast
description: The perfect energy-boosting breakfast for early morning lectures.
steps: Toast the bread slices.
Spread peanut butter evenly on each slice.
Top with banana slices.
Drizzle honey if desired.

title: Oatmeal with Berries
description: Heart-healthy breakfast that keeps you full until lunch.
steps: Boil milk or water in a small pot.
Add oats and simmer for 5 minutes.
Pour into a bowl and top with berries.
Drizzle honey on top.

title: Masala Omelette
description: A spicy, protein-packed start to your day.
steps: Whisk eggs with onion, chili, turmeric, and salt.
Heat oil in a pan.
Pour egg mixture and cook for 2 mins per side.
Fold and serve with toast.

title: Chickpea Salad
description: Fresh, crunchy, and incredibly cheap. Great for lunch between classes.
steps: Rinse chickpeas and place in a bowl.
Add diced cucumber and tomato.
Mix in lemon juice, olive oil, salt, and pepper.
Toss and serve chilled.

title: Tuna Sandwich
description: Quick protein hit. No cooking required!
steps: Mix tuna with mayonnaise in a bowl.
Place lettuce on a slice of bread.
Spread tuna mixture over lettuce.
Cover with second slice of bread.

title: Vegetable Stir-Fry
description: Clean out your fridge with this versatile stir-fry.
steps: Heat oil and sauté ginger.
Add frozen vegetables and stir-fry on high heat.
Pour in soy sauce and toss until glazed.
Serve as is or with rice.

title: Paneer Bhurji
description: Scrambled cottage cheese with spices. A protein-rich vegetarian delight.
steps: Heat oil and sauté cumin and onions.
Add tomatoes and cook until soft.
Stir in chili powder and crumbled paneer.
Cook for 5 minutes and garnish with coriander.

title: Greek Yogurt Parfait
description: Refreshing and high in probiotics. Perfect for a quick snack.
steps: Spoon yogurt into a glass.
Add a layer of granola.
Top with fresh berries.
Drizzle with honey.

title: Mushroom Toast
description: Sophisticated but simple. Great for a weekend brunch.
steps: Sauté garlic in butter.
Add mushrooms and cook until brown.
Toast the bread slices.
Place mushrooms on toast and season.

title: Lentil Soup (Dal)
description: Pure nutrition. One of the most affordable and healthy meals globally.
steps: Boil lentils with water and turmeric until mushy.
In a separate pan, sauté garlic in oil.
Pour the garlic oil into the boiled lentils.
Stir and simmer for 5 minutes.

title: Quick Quesadilla
description: Cheesy and satisfying. Perfect for a midnight snack.
steps: Butter one side of the tortillas.
Place one tortilla butter-side down in a pan.
Sprinkle cheese and beans on top.
Cover with second tortilla and flip when golden brown.
